steps.label: fe_indexed_history
steps.stdout_tail:
========================================================================
AXIS 0 Fe-INDEXED Xi_hist PROBE
========================================================================
Comparing Fe-indexed bridge constructions against Phase 4 winner.

  A = Phase 4 winner (baseline): full trajectory, exp weighting
  B = Fe-indexed 7-step window: 8 windows at Fe steps, MI-weighted
  C = Fe pairs only: Ti→Fe adjacent pairs, MI-weighted
  D = lag=7 pairs: compression horizon lag, no Fe indexing

  1/inner: A=1.7457 B=1.5390 C=1.8419 D=1.2404 | winner=C_fe_pairs_only gain=+0.0962
  1/clifford: A=1.1540 B=0.6755 C=1.0111 D=0.6672 | winner=A_phase4_winner gain=+0.0000
  1/outer: A=1.7458 B=1.5390 C=1.8419 D=1.2404 | winner=C_fe_pairs_only gain=+0.0961
  2/inner: A=1.6219 B=1.7942 C=1.8394 D=1.5580 | winner=C_fe_pairs_only gain=+0.2175
  2/clifford: A=1.0082 B=1.4245 C=1.4462 D=1.1587 | winner=C_fe_pairs_only gain=+0.4380
  2/outer: A=1.6217 B=1.7942 C=1.8394 D=1.5580 | winner=C_fe_pairs_only gain=+0.2177

========================================================================
OVERALL RESULTS
========================================================================
  Fe-step MI advantage over non-Fe: +0.1174

  Bridge                mean MI  mean Ic  gain vs A  wins
  -------------------------------------------------------
  A Phase4 baseline      1.4829   0.4926          —     1/6
  B Fe-indexed 7-win     1.4611   0.4725    -0.0218     0/6
  C Fe pairs only        1.6366   0.6446    +0.1538     5/6
  D lag=7 pairs          1.2371   0.2586    -0.2458     0/6

  ✓ KEEP — C_fe_pairs_only beats Phase 4 winner by +0.1538 MI
    Fe-indexed construction provides meaningful improvement.

  FEP framing:
    Fe steps = 2/32 = 25% of trajectory
    Fe MI mean: 1.5956
    Non-Fe MI: 1.4782
    Interpretation: Fe is prediction INSTANTIATION, not sensory correction.
    Phase5A certified marginal-preserving MI ≈ 0 → prediction dominates.
    The entire 4-step cycle is the prior unfolding; Fe is its peak.

================================================================================
